text.title: Discovery interview question set
text.summary: Eight grouped questions the orchestrator MUST ask before proposing a format. Discrete-option groups use AskUserQuestion; free-text groups are asked plainly. All answers are gathered before any output.

rule.statement: The orchestrator MUST gather answers to ALL eight question groups before proposing any format, scaffold, or architecture.
rule.rationale: Identity, language scope, automation level, and source mix interact non-linearly. Proposing after the third answer locks in a complexity level that will not match later answers, forcing rework.

section.title: Q1: Media identity
section.p: Single-select. Header: "Media Identity". Question: "What media are you creating?"

list.item: News outlet — breaking news, reports, analysis on a topic or region.
Expert blog — one voice, deep expertise in a niche.
Community media — for a specific community (diaspora, profession, hobby).
Brand media — company blog, thought leadership, product updates.
Digest or curation — curate and summarize from other sources.

section.title: Q2: Topic and audience
section.p: Free text. Ask all four sub-questions in one turn.

list.item: What topics do you cover? List three to ten.
Who is your reader? Age, profession, interests, pain points.
What languages? Which is primary?
What region or city if location-specific?

section.title: Q3: Content strategy
section.p: Multi-select. Header: "Content Types". Question: "What content types do you need?"

list.item: Breaking news (300-600 words).
In-depth materials (600-1500 words).
Practical guides (800-2000 words).
Opinion or analysis (600-1200 words).
Daily digest (compilation).
Utility alerts (weather, transport, etc.).

section.p: Follow-up free-text: how many articles per day, what publishing schedule.

section.title: Q4: Channels and platform
section.p: Multi-select. Header: "Channels". Question: "Where do you publish?"

list.item: Telegram channel.
Website (static site).
Both Telegram plus website.
Newsletter (email).
Social media (Twitter/X, Instagram).

section.title: Q5: Brand voice
section.p: Free text. Ask as one block.

list.item: Author name or pseudonym (real-sounding or character).
Tone: formal, warm, ironic, academic, casual.
Specific voice rules (for example, explain terms for immigrants).
Visual style for images: photo, illustration, comic, minimalist.

section.title: Q6: Content sources
section.p: Multi-select. Header: "Sources". Question: "Where does content come from?"

list.item: RSS feeds (news sites).
Web search (Google, SearXNG).
APIs (weather, transport, government).
Manual input (editor notes).
Social media monitoring.

section.title: Q7: Automation level
section.p: Single-select. Header: "Automation". Question: "How automated should it be?"

list.item: Full auto-pilot — generate and publish without human review.
Generate plus review before publish — human approves through admin panel.
Semi-auto — human writes, AI assists with research and editing.
Manual with AI tools — human drives, AI helps on demand.

section.title: Q8: Technical
section.p: Free text. Ask as one block.

list.item: Domain name (existing or new).
Hosting server available (faion-net, own server, or need a new one).
Telegram bot (existing shared bot or create new through @BotFather).
Budget for APIs (Claude, OpenAI image generation).
